Today is February 22, 2026. An armed man was shot dead early this morning after entering the secure perimeter of President Trump's Mar-a-Lago residence. According to authorities, what specific items was the suspect carrying when he was intercepted?
(A) A shotgun and a fuel can
(B) A rifle and a backpack containing explosives
(C) A handgun and a canister of gasoline
(D) A shotgun and a hunting knife
(E) A semi-automatic weapon and a propane tank
explanation: The article states the suspect, Austin T Martin, was "carrying what appeared to be a shotgun and a fuel can" when he was observed by the north gate.
model_output:
Based on recent news reports from today (February 22, 2026), an armed man in his early 20s breached the north gate of Mar-a-Lago around 1:30 a.m. and was shot dead by law enforcement (a sheriff's deputy and Secret Service agents) after he dropped a gas can but raised a shotgun toward them. The items he was carrying match option (A).

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [FOX8LIVE] (https://www.fox8live.com/2026/02/22/law-enforcement-shoots-kills-man-mar-a-lago-who-breached-gate-with-shotgun-gas-can)[[1]](https://www.fox8live.com/2026/02/22/law-enforcement-shoots-kills-man-mar-a-lago-who-breached-gate-with-shotgun-gas-can)

Today is February 22, 2026. The United States men's ice hockey team has just won Olympic gold for the first time in 46 years. How long into the overtime period did Jack Hughes score the winning goal against Canada?
(A) 46 seconds
(B) 101 seconds
(C) 80 seconds
(D) 121 seconds
(E) 6 minutes
explanation: The article explicitly mentions that "USA scored the winner 101 seconds into overtime."
model_output:
The United States defeated Canada 2-1 in the gold medal game of the 2026 Winter Olympics men's ice hockey tournament, with the winning goal coming in overtime. According to reports, Jack Hughes scored the goal at 1:41 into the overtime period[[1]](https://www.nhl.com/news/united-states-canada-2026-olympics-gold-medal-game-recap-february-22-2026), which equates to 101 seconds. This was further confirmed by extracting details directly from the NHL.com recap[[1]](https://www.nhl.com/news/united-states-canada-2026-olympics-gold-medal-game-recap-february-22-2026).

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [NHL.com] (https://www.nhl.com/news/united-states-canada-2026-olympics-gold-medal-game-recap-february-22-2026)
- [YouTube] (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8c47m212Mkk)
- [Olympics.com] (https://www.olympics.com/en/milano-cortina-2026/news/winter-olympics-2026-united-states-defeat-canada-overtime)

Today is February 22, 2026. Authorities in California have confirmed that all nine missing individuals from the Lake Tahoe avalanche have been found dead. According to Sheriff Shannan Moon, what was the composition of the group of victims?
(A) Five tourists, two local residents, and two professional skiers
(B) Six experienced backcountry skiers and three local volunteers
(C) Six friends (including a pair of sisters) and three mountain guides
(D) Seven members of a ski club and two park rangers
(E) Four family members and five professional guides
explanation: The article states the victims "included close friends, a pair of sisters and three guides."
model_output:
The details from recent news reports indicate that the nine victims were part of a larger group of 15 backcountry skiers on a guided tour. The deceased consisted of six close friends (passionate and experienced skiers, including sisters Liz Clabaugh and Caroline Sekar) and three professional mountain guides from Blackbird Mountain Guides. This aligns with statements from authorities, including Sheriff Shannan Moon, who confirmed the recovery of all nine bodies and provided updates on the incident.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [KOLO] (https://www.kolotv.com/2026/02/21/bodies-all-9-avalanche-victims-have-been-recovered-sheriff-said)[[1]](https://www.kolotv.com/2026/02/21/bodies-all-9-avalanche-victims-have-been-recovered-sheriff-said)
- [NBC News] (https://www.nbcnews.com/news/us-news/lake-tahoe-avalanche-victim-names-rcna260068)[[2]](https://www.nbcnews.com/news/us-news/lake-tahoe-avalanche-victim-names-rcna260068)

===============================================

Today is February 22, 2026. NASA has announced that the Artemis II lunar mission will no longer launch on March 6. What specific technical issue was observed by engineers overnight on Friday that led to this delay?
(A) A hydrogen leak in the filters and seals of the main engine
(B) A software glitch in the navigation system during the "wet rehearsal"
(C) A structural crack in the propellant tank discovered during fueling
(D) An interruption in the flow of helium used for pressurization and cooling
(E) A failure in the communication link between the rocket and Kennedy Space Center
explanation: The article states that "engineers observed an interruption in the flow of helium required for launch operations."
model_output:
Based on recent news reports from NASA and various outlets, the Artemis II mission delay was triggered by a helium flow issue detected overnight on Friday. This aligns directly with option (D), as multiple sources describe an interruption in the helium flow used for pressurization and cooling in the rocket's upper stage (specifically the SLS interim cryogenic propulsion stage). Previous issues like hydrogen leaks had been addressed, but this new helium problem necessitated the delay and likely a rollback from the launchpad for repairs, pushing back the planned March 6 launch.

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- BBC ([https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c626v265zqlo])
- Space.com ([https://www.space.com/space-exploration/artemis/problem-pops-up-with-artemis-2-moon-rocket-this-will-almost-assuredly-impact-the-march-launch-window])
- Facebook/NASA ([https://www.facebook.com/NASA/posts/as-soon-as-tuesday-feb-24-we-will-roll-our-moon-rocket-for-artemis-ii-mission-of/1461814105313961])

- We Are Iowa ([https://www.weareiowa.com/article/news/nation-world/artemis-launch-window-date-update-helium-flow-issue-nasa/507-db3b2d74-60ec-43b2-b977-3131d663e9c8])
- Times of India ([https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/science/new-glitch-hits-moon-mission-why-did-nasa-delay-artemis-ii-again/articleshow/128664841.cms])

===============================================

Today is February 22, 2026. President Trump has announced an increase in his proposed global tariffs following a Supreme Court ruling. Under which specific law does the administration intend to impose the new 15% temporary tariff?
(A) The International Emergency Economic Powers Act (IEEPA) of 1977
(B) Section 232 of the Trade Expansion Act of 1962
(C) The North American Free Trade Agreement Implementation Act
(D) Section 301 of the Trade Act of 1974
(E) Section 122 of the 1974 Trade Act
explanation: The article mentions the new rate is a "temporary solution under Section 122 of the 1974 Trade Act."
model_output:
First, a quick recap of the scenario: On February 22, 2026 (today), President Trump announced an increase in global tariffs to a 15% temporary rate, following a Supreme Court ruling that apparently struck down his ability to impose tariffs under some other authority. The question is which specific law he's using for this new 15% temporary tariff.

Based on current reporting from multiple sources, the administration is invoking Section 122 of the Trade Act of 1974 for this purpose. This section allows the president to impose temporary tariffs of up to 15% (for up to 150 days) to address balance-of-payments issues. It fits the description of a "temporary" global tariff, and reports indicate Trump initially set it at 10% under this law before raising it to 15% in response to the court ruling.[[1]](https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/2/22/trump-tariff-chaos-what-does-15-levy-mean-for-trade-deals-the-us-signed)[[2]](https://www.reuters.com/world/us/trump-says-he-will-raise-global-tariff-rate-10-15-2026-02-21)[[3]](https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/supreme-court/live-blog/-trump-tariffs-ruling-supreme-court-live-updates-rcna252655)[[4]](https://www.politico.com/news/2026/02/20/donald-trump-10-percent-global-tariff-00791317)[[5]](https://theconversation.com/trump-hikes-global-tariffs-to-15-as-the-fallout-from-supreme-court-loss-continues-273105)[[6]](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/live/tariff-ruling-live-coverage-trump-attacks-supreme-court-imposes-10-global-tariff-then-raises-it-to-15-184403258.html)

The other options don't align:
- (A) IEEPA is for national emergencies and has been used for sanctions, but it's not cited here.
- (B) Section 232 is for national security-based tariffs, but the Supreme Court ruling appears to have limited unilateral presidential power in a related context.
- (C) The NAFTA Implementation Act is about trade agreements, not broad tariff imposition.
- (D) Section 301 is for responding to unfair trade practices, but again, not the law referenced in the announcement.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [PBS] (https://www.pbs.org/newshour/economy/president-trump-increases-global-tariffs-to-15-after-supreme-court-decision)
- [CNN] (https://www.cnn.com/us/live-news/trump-administration-tariffs-iran-news-02-21-26)
- [Al Jazeera] (https://www.aljazeera.com/news/2026/2/22/trump-tariff-chaos-what-does-15-levy-mean-for-trade-deals-the-us-signed)
- [Reuters] (https://www.reuters.com/world/us/trump-says-he-will-raise-global-tariff-rate-10-15-2026-02-21)
- [NBC News] (https://www.nbcnews.com/politics/supreme-court/live-blog/-trump-tariffs-ruling-supreme-court-live-updates-rcna252655)
- [Politico] (https://www.politico.com/news/2026/02/20/donald-trump-10-percent-global-tariff-00791317)
- [NPR] (https://www.npr.org/2026/02/21/nx-s1-5722111/trump-global-tariffs-15-percent)
- [The Conversation] (https://theconversation.com/trump-hikes-global-tariffs-to-15-as-the-fallout-from-supreme-court-loss-continues-273105)
- [Yahoo Finance] (https://finance.yahoo.com/news/live/tariff-ruling-live-coverage-trump-attacks-supreme-court-imposes-10-global-tariff-then-raises-it-to-15-184403258.html)

Today is February 22, 2026. Salsa pioneer Willie Colón has passed away at the age of 75. In 1967, at the age of 16, with whom did he record his first album, "El Malo"?
(A) Rubén Blades
(B) Héctor Lavoe
(C) Celia Cruz
(D) Ismael Miranda
(E) Bad Bunny
explanation: The article states: "In 1967, at 16, he recorded his first album El Malo with Héctor Lavoe."
model_output:
The salsa pioneer Willie Colón indeed passed away recently, as noted in the query (on February 21, 2026, per recent reports). Regarding his first album, *El Malo* (released in 1967 on Fania Records), it was recorded when he was 16 years old and featured Héctor Lavoe on vocals. This marked the beginning of their iconic collaboration in the salsa genre.

<final_answer>
(B)
</final_answer>

Today is February 22, 2026. Details have emerged regarding the suspect in the Tumbler Ridge mass shooting, Jesse Van Rootselaar. According to police reports, who were among the eight victims killed in the attack?
(A) Eight students at the Tumbler Ridge Secondary School
(B) Three teachers and five staff members of the school
(C) The suspect's mother and step-brother
(D) A group of OpenAI employees visiting British Columbia
(E) Twelve students and fifteen local residents
explanation: The article states: "Van Rootselaars's mother and step-brother were among the victims of the shooting. Both were found dead at a local residence, police said."
model_output:
The details indicate that the mass shooting involved eight victims killed (excluding the suspect, who died by suicide). This included two killed at a residence before the suspect proceeded to Tumbler Ridge Secondary School: the suspect's mother (Jennifer Jacobs, per some reports; Jennifer Strang per others) and 11-year-old step-brother (Emmett Jacobs, per some reports; described as half-brother in others). The remaining six victims were killed at the school (five students and one education assistant). Among the options, (C) aligns with the confirmed victims.

<final_answer>
(C)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:

Today is February 22, 2026. President Trump has indicated he is considering a limited military strike on Iran. According to his statement on Thursday, within what timeframe will the world find out if a deal is reached or if military action will be taken?
(A) Over the next 10 days
(B) Within the next 48 hours
(C) By the end of the month
(D) Within the next two weeks
(E) Over the next 150 days
explanation: The article states: "On Thursday, Trump said the world would find out 'over the next, probably, 10 days' whether a deal would be reached or the US would take military action."
model_output:
First, I determined that February 22, 2026, is a Sunday, so the Thursday statement was on February 19, 2026.

Then, I searched for the statement and found consistent reporting that Trump's exact words were: "You’ll be finding out over the next probably 10 days." He later elaborated that 10-15 days was the maximum timeframe.

The phrasing in the main quote directly matches option (A), and multiple sources summarize the timeframe as 10 days.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

</final_answer>

However, the specific tactic used on Friday (February 20) before searching for (and ultimately recovering) the final victim was pouring water from helicopters to stabilize the snowpack.

(C)

Today is February 22, 2026. Several contributors to the Project 2025 document now hold positions within the Trump administration. Which of the following groups of individuals are identified in the article as Project 2025 contributors currently in the administration?
(A) John Ratcliffe, Brendan Carr, Tom Homan, Paul Atkins, and Peter Navarro
(B) Russell Vought, Paul Dans, Eugene Kiley, and James Goodwin
(C) Nicolás Maduro, John Ratcliffe, and Tom Homan
(D) Todd Blanche, Erik Siebert, and Lindsey Halligan
(E) John Ratcliffe, Brendan Carr, Jamieson Greer, and Scott Bessent
explanation: The article lists: "CIA Director John Ratcliffe; Brendan Carr, the Federal Communications Commission boss; Tom Homan, Trump's 'border tsar'; Paul Atkins, chair of the Securities and Exchange Commission; and trade adviser Peter Navarro..."
model_output:
### Reasoning

The question refers to "the article" without specifying which one, but based on the current date (February 22, 2026), I used web search and page browsing tools to identify relevant recent sources discussing Project 2025 contributors in the Trump administration. Key findings:

- A BBC article (dated ~20 hours ago as of the query) explicitly lists John Ratcliffe (CIA Director), Brendan Carr (FCC boss), Tom Homan (border tsar), Paul Atkins (SEC Chair), Peter Navarro (trade adviser), and Russell Vought (OMB Director) as Project 2025 contributors now in the administration.
- This matches option (A) exactly for all five names.
- Other options partially overlap or include non-matches:
  - (B) Includes Vought (matches) but Paul Dans (Project 2025 director, not in admin per sources), Eugene Kiley, and James Goodwin (not mentioned).
  - (C) Includes Ratcliffe and Homan (matches) but Nicolás Maduro (irrelevant, not a contributor).
  - (D) Names not mentioned as Project 2025 contributors in sources.
  - (E) Includes Ratcliffe and Carr (matches) but Jamieson Greer (Trade Rep, not linked to Project 2025) and Scott Bessent (Treasury Secretary, not linked).
- An ABC News article lists a different subset (Vought, Hoekstra, Miller, Carr, Ratcliffe, Homan), but doesn't match any option fully. No single source matches other options as cleanly as the BBC article does for (A).

Thus, option (A) is the best match for a group identified in a relevant article.

<final_answer>
(A)
</final_answer>

CITATIONS:
- [BBC News] (https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c5yvvjw8pdvo)
- [ABC News] (https://abcnews.go.com/Politics/trump-project-2025-administration/story?id=116019369)

(A) James W. Hundley
(B) Todd Blanche
(C) Lindsey Halligan
(D) Erik Siebert
(E) James Comey
explanation: The article states: "Hundley was picked to fill the seat vacated by Erik Siebert, who resigned in September under pressure from President Donald Trump... Siebert's office ultimately decided not to bring criminal charges against James..."
model_output:
The events described appear to be part of a sequence in late 2025 and early 2026 involving Trump administration pressure on federal prosecutors in the Eastern District of Virginia. Based on available information, Erik Siebert was the acting U.S. attorney who resigned in September 2025 amid pressure to bring mortgage fraud charges against Letitia James, despite reported concerns over insufficient evidence. He chose to step down rather than pursue the case. This set off a chain of subsequent appointments and conflicts, including the brief tenure of Lindsey Halligan (who later resigned due to judicial pushback on her appointment's legitimacy) and the firing of James W. Hundley (who was appointed by judges but quickly ousted by the DOJ).

<final_answer>
(D)
</final_answer>

Today is February 22, 2026. Trade negotiations between the US and Canada were halted last October. What specific event triggered President Trump to stop the talks?
(A) Canada's refusal to sell US wine and spirits on their shelves.
(B) The implementation of the Online Streaming Act by the Canadian government.
(C) A disagreement over dairy import rules during the USMCA review.
(D) A social media post by Prime Minister Mark Carney from Quebec.
(E) An anti-tariffs advert sponsored by the province of Ontario that aired during the World Series.
explanation: The article states: "...talks were halted last October by Trump, who was upset over an anti-tariffs advert sponsored by Canada's province of Ontario that aired on US networks - including during the World Series."
model_output:
Based on web search results, the trade negotiations between the US and Canada were halted by President Trump in October 2025 due to an anti-tariff advertisement sponsored by the province of Ontario. This ad, which featured Ronald Reagan warning about tariffs, aired during the World Series and was cited by Trump as interference in US affairs, prompting him to terminate the talks[[1]](https://www.nbcnews.com/business/media/trump-canada-trade-world-series-ad-rcna239669)[[2]](https://www.cnn.com/2025/10/23/politics/trump-ends-trade-negotiations-canada)[[3]](https://www.reuters.com/world/us/trump-says-all-trade-talks-with-canada-are-terminated-2025-10-24). This directly matches option (E). Other options, such as dairy disputes or the Online Streaming Act, do not align with the reported trigger event.

<final_answer>
(E)
</final_answer>
